title: haloragaceae
intro: The Haloragaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as water-dilloes or water milfoils. This family consists of approximately 56 species of plants that are primarily found in aquatic environments, such as ponds, lakes, and slow-moving streams. Characterized by their distinctive leaf shapes and adaptations to water environments, the Haloragaceae family is found in many parts of the world, including Australia, New Zealand, and parts of Asia and North America. The plants within this family often have submerged or floating leaves, and are often used as indicators of water quality due to their sensitivity to environmental changes.
